description: Machine-readable Alembic internal layer contract (P2 AD3, Core CO2 pattern). Codifies the AS-IS lib/ cross-area runtime import graph derived on 2026-06-12 via scripts/lint-layer-contract.mjs --report at baseline 9bcc272 (194 runtime edges, 49 type-only bridges) — the contract records reality; redesigns go through controller-decided waves. Enforced by scripts/lint-layer-contract.mjs as a blocking step in npm run check. Imports resolve through both relative specifiers and the package.json '#alias/*' subpath map.
typeOnlyNote: import type / export type imports are type bridges and do not create runtime coupling; runtime imports must follow allowedRuntimeImports.
layerRationale: Base leaves (import nothing or shared only): shared, types, platform, infrastructure, governance, project-scope, repository, sandbox, generated.
Mid: service (domain orchestration over daemon contracts/infrastructure), tools (host adapters over sandbox), workflows (cold-start/rescan flows over service).

Injection is the DI/composition area: it wires daemon, repositories, services, and tools; many areas also reach INTO it via the service-locator pattern (getAiRuntimeStatus/getServiceContainer) — those reach-through edges are codified as-is here and are AD4 remediation scope, not silently legal forever.

Hosts: http, resident, daemon, cli sit on top and may reach the areas they observably consume; lib/bootstrap.ts ('root') is the composition entry with a wildcard, and cli may invoke it (SetupService bootstraps the app).
generated holds committed codegen artifacts (dashboard api-types) and imports nothing.

blessedImports.reason: Minority side of the injection<->service cycle (injection->service x10 is the layering; this single getAiRuntimeStatus reach-through is the inversion). Service-locator pattern pending port injection.
Minority side of the injection<->tools cycle (injection->tools x10 is the layering; this single getAiRuntimeStatus/getAiUnavailableMessage reach-through is the inversion).
Minority side of the http<->resident cycle (http->resident x3 is the layering; the submit rate-limit gate lives in http/middleware/RateLimiter and resident handlers reach it dynamically).
blessedImports.cleanupTrigger: AD4/AD5: move the shared rate-limit capability out of the http host area (infrastructure or service) so both hosts consume it downward

blessedImports.reason: Same http<->resident cycle minority side as knowledge.ts (dynamic RateLimiter import in the submit pipeline).

cycleFindings.majority: http -> resident (3 edges: route modules invoke resident handlers)
cycleFindings.minority: resident -> http (2 edges: dynamic RateLimiter imports, blessed above)
cycleFindings.status: recorded 2026-06-12; resolution owned by AD4/AD5 RateLimiter relocation

cycleFindings.minority: service -> injection (1 edge: ModuleService getAiRuntimeStatus, blessed above)
cycleFindings.status: recorded 2026-06-12; resolution owned by AD4 service-locator remediation

cycleFindings.minority: tools -> injection (1 edge: DashboardOperations getAiRuntimeStatus, blessed above)
cycleFindings.status: recorded 2026-06-12; resolution owned by AD4 service-locator remediation
